item.title: Phase1-file-workflow: New file, recent files, and unsaved-changes prompt
item.intent: The user can create new untitled documents, reopen recently-used files from the menu, and is never asked to lose unsaved work without a prompt. The M0 single-file open/save loop must grow into a polished daily-use workflow.
item.description:
Extend the M0 file handling (FEAT-005) with three capabilities:

1. **New file** (Cmd+N): opens a new untitled buffer in a new Tauri window. Each window is independent — one file per window, consistent with the DP-1 no-vault principle.

2. **Recent files**: File > Open Recent submenu populated from a local config file (~/.config/markdown/recent.json or the Tauri app data dir). Track the last 10 opened file paths. On click, open the file in a new window.

3. **Unsaved-changes prompt**: when the user attempts to close a window, open a different file, or open a new file while the current document has unsaved changes, show a native Tauri dialog: 'Save changes to [filename]?' with Save / Discard / Cancel buttons. Save writes the file; Discard discards; Cancel aborts the action.

Recent file tracking: update the list on every successful file open and save. Persist immediately to disk. Paths that no longer exist on disk are silently removed from the list on next load.
steps: Add a Tauri command `get_recent_files() -> Vec<String>` that reads ~/.config/markdown/recent.json (or equivalent app data path)
Add a Tauri command `add_recent_file(path: String)` that prepends the path to the list and trims to 10 entries
Build the File > Open Recent submenu in the Tauri menu builder using the recent file list
Implement a new-window command triggered by Cmd+N: create a new Tauri WebviewWindow with a fresh untitled document state
Track dirty state in the frontend: set a hasUnsavedChanges flag on every editor change, clear it on save
Intercept the window close event (Tauri onCloseRequested); if hasUnsavedChanges, show a native dialog before allowing close
Persist recent files on every successful open and save
